## [06:03 - 09:09]

$30 and you say, right, I want to go in at my lowest cost to be capped at $30. It means that if there's a chance of a conversion happening here and Facebook predicts that the conversion will cost more than $30, it just won't bid. So the way I use this is let's say for example, my CPA target is $30 and I've done some testing where I found that the $90 I've gone tested $30, $40, $50, maybe up to $90 and my $90 is the best before we're on a cap. Then I might test different boundaries on my target cost at $80, $70 where I get, I want an average bid at that level and Facebook will try and bring my CPA within that as well. So quite often what Facebook will tell you to do is your target CPA should be the actual CPA you're after. So if you're after $30 CPA, set that as your target cost, sometimes it'll be higher, sometimes it'll be lower.
